reasoning: There is currently no vaccine for Chagas disease (American trypanosomiasis) listed on the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page. Extensive searches confirm that there are no vaccines available or close to approval for Chagas disease. The leading organization developing treatments, DNDi, states in an early 2025 update that they aim to launch a Phase III trial for a Chagas vaccine candidate by 2028. Because vaccine development, especially Phase III trials, takes several years, it is impossible for a vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia article by any of the 2025 or 2026 resolution dates. The probability remains at the absolute minimum (0.02) for the near-term dates and slightly higher (0.05) for 2026 only to account for theoretically possible but extraordinarily unlikely definitions or Wikipedia vandalism events, aligning with the rule that uncertainty should increase over time.
